page: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The configuration features 32 vCPUs, which correspond to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache, alongside support for advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and cryptography instructions like AES and SHA_NI. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the E32as_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth write speeds fall within the average range. The system shows strong capabilities in PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption, which place it in the top 25%. For specialized tasks, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small (135M) language models, though performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% when executing text generation on large (70B) models due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, the E32as_v7 offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The instance is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data compression pipelines, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor. It delivers 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 4.5 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation. While it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU scores. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain average, and large-model LLM text generation is poor due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This profile makes the E32as_v7 highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as databases, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, built for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine delivering high memory density and strong single-core performance for demanding database and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz.
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded data compression performance.
No bundled local storage or GPU, but supports Premium Storage.
Optimal for memory-intensive workloads, databases, and in-memory caching.
